current_question: How are leading labs operationalizing their RSPs/safety frameworks in practice? What's the state of dangerous capability evals for current frontier models?
filter_outs: Pure capabilities work with no safety angle, speculative long-term alignment philosophy with no near-term connection, ML theory unconnected to safety
freeform: Senior AI safety researcher with a broad portfolio. Track alignment (scalable oversight, RLHF successors, debate, weak-to-strong generalization), evaluation and red-teaming (dangerous capability evals, deception evals, model organisms), AI governance and policy, and security. Want signal across the whole field, prefer practical safety work over philosophy.
